reports.text:
BenQ India MD Rajeev Singh: Targeting 25% Annual Growth for Next 5 Years; Projectors & India-Designed Products to Drive Next Phase

Growth Strategy
Targeting 25% annual growth sustained over the next 5 years

Growth driven by flat-panel displays, computer monitors, and projectors (commercial + personal)

Deliberate focus on mid- and high-range segments вАФ avoiding entry-level due to low value addition

Consumer behaviour shift from first-time purchases to repeat buying on value, supporting premium positioning

Projector Segment вАФ Key Bet
Projectors called "most underrated product category" вАФ expected to evolve significantly, especially in home segment

Unlike TVs (fixed position), projectors are portable вАФ strong appeal to younger demographic

India projector market could double annually over next few years

Projector segment just beginning to take off in India and globally

Make in India вЖТ Designed for India
Strategy evolving from 'Make in India' to 'Designed for India'

Two contract manufacturers in Gujarat and Tirupati (Andhra Pradesh); may invest in own facility if needed

Coding monitors fully researched in India (Hyderabad + Bengaluru) вАФ engaging programmers to understand local needs

Goal: develop India-first products not in international portfolio вАФ "if it succeeds in India, it can do well globally"

Core Theme
BenQ India is executing a deliberate premiumisation play вАФ anchoring in mid-to-high displays while betting heavily on projectors as the next mass category, and shifting from contract manufacturing to India-originated R&D, positioning India as both a high-growth market and a global product innovation hub.

Bank of Baroda Q4FY26: Net Profit Up 11.2% YoY to вВє5,616 Crore on NII Growth; Asset Quality Improves to Best-Ever GNPA of 1.89%

Q4FY26 Headline Numbers
Net Profit: вВє5,616 crore, up 11.2% YoY

NII: вВє12,494 crore, up 8.7% YoY

Non-interest income: вВє3,967 crore, down 16.2% YoY

NIM: 2.89% in Q4FY26 vs 2.98% in Q4FY25

Full-year FY26 net profit: вВє20,021 crore, up 2.2% YoY

Loan Growth
Domestic advances: +14.5% YoY to вВє11.69 trillion

Retail segment: +17.9% YoY to вВє3.03 trillion

Corporate loans: +11.2% YoY to вВє4.57 trillion

MSME loans: +15.6% YoY to ~вВє1.60 trillion

Asset Quality вАФ Improving
GNPA ratio: 1.89% (Mar '26) vs 2.04% (Dec '25) vs 2.26% (Mar '25)

Net NPA: 0.45% (Mar '26) vs 0.57% (Dec '25) vs 0.58% (Mar '25)

Floating provision of вВє1,500 crore made during Q4; credit cost at 0.76% vs 0.44% in Q4FY25

Deposits
Domestic deposits: +12.8% YoY to вВє14.01 trillion

CASA ratio: 38.90% vs 39.97% a year ago вАФ slight dilution

ECLGS 5.0 Opportunity
MSME book ~вВє1.60 trillion; 55-60% is working capital

Up to 20% of working capital eligible under ECLGS вАФ translates to вВє12,000 crore+ disbursement potential

ECL impact still being computed; management guided it will come in well below earlier estimates

Core Theme 
BoB delivered a clean Q4 with NII-led profit growth and its best-ever asset quality metrics, while flagging NIM compression from elevated credit costs and a floating provision build вАФ the ECLGS 5.0 pipeline of вВє12,000 crore offers a near-term MSME growth catalyst as the bank navigates the ECL transition.

TSMC Sales Jump 17.5% as Global AI Chip Spending Accelerates

TSMC reported a 17.5% rise in April sales to NT$410.7 billion ($13.1 billion)

Growth driven by sustained AI infrastructure spending by hyperscalers

Company manufactures advanced chips for Nvidia and AMD, central to the AI boom

Tech giants including Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Alphabet are set to spend $725 billion on AI this year

TSMC raised its full-year guidance and expects capex to trend toward the upper end of its $56 billion plan

Strong AI demand continues to offset weakness in smartphone and consumer electronics markets

Respiratory Drug Sales Surge 18% Amid Spike in Asthma and Flu Cases

Respiratory drug sales rose 17.7% YoY in April 2026 to вВє1,632 crore

Growth outpaced overall Indian pharma market growth of 10.3%

Demand driven by seasonal flu, rising asthma cases, and pollution-related respiratory issues

Anti-asthma and COPD drugs recorded 22% growth despite summer season

Nasal preparations and cough-cold medicines also saw strong demand growth

Doctors cite poor air quality, dust exposure, temperature swings, and pollution as key triggers

Highlights growing healthcare burden from environmental and respiratory conditions

Titan Q4FY26: Net Profit Jumps 35% to вВє1,179 Crore; Revenue Surges 46% to вВє20,300 Crore as Jewellery & Watches Fire on All Cylinders

Headline Numbers
Net Profit: вВє1,179 crore, up 35% YoY

Total Income: вВє20,300 crore, up 46% YoY

FY26 full-year total income: вВє76,078 crore, up 33% YoY

FY26 full-year PAT: вВє5,073 crore, up 52% YoY

Segment-Wise Revenue
Jewellery: вВє18,195 crore вАФ up 50% YoY (excl. bullion & digi-gold)

Watches (Analog): вВє18,195 crore вАФ up 15% YoY, driven by premiumisation

Eyewear: вВє227 crore вАФ up 17% YoY

Jewellery вАФ Deep Dive
India jewellery business (Tanishq + Mia + Zoya): вВє17,114 crore, up 46% YoY

Combined domestic portfolio grew 48% to вВє16,047 crore

CaratLane: вВє1,066 crore, up 22% YoY

International jewellery: вВє1,081 crore, up 174% YoY вАФ double-digit retail growth in GCC and North America

Strategic Move вАФ Damas Acquisition
Completed 67% stake in Damas Jewellery (GCC market)

Added 123 stores to Titan's network вАФ significant overseas expansion milestone

Also opened 4 new stores in GCC during the quarter

Retail Expansion
27 new stores added in India вАФ 8 Tanishq, 14 Mia, 5 CaratLane

Tanishq also expanded in GCC region

Weak Spot
Smart watches: nearly 50% decline in overall value вАФ category remains under pressure

Milestone
Crossed вВє50,000 crore annual revenue in FY25 after 40 years вАФ then added next вВє25,000 crore in a single year (FY26)

Core Theme
Titan delivered a landmark FY26 вАФ jewellery momentum at elevated gold prices, analog watch premiumisation, and the transformative Damas acquisition collectively re-rated the growth curve; the smartwatch drag is the only blemish in an otherwise broad-based quarter that sets up aggressive international expansion as the next compounding lever.

Oil PSUs Face вВє30,000 Cr Monthly Under-Recoveries Amid Fuel Price Freeze

State-run oil companies are suffering ~вВє30,000 crore monthly under-recoveries

Losses stem from selling petrol, diesel, and LPG below international benchmark prices

Global crude and fuel prices surged following the Iran-West Asia conflict

Retail petrol and diesel prices remain largely unchanged despite rising procurement costs

Shares of Indian Oil, HPCL, and BPCL have fallen 12вАУ23% since late February

Commercial LPG and aviation fuel prices have risen sharply, while domestic fuel hikes remain limited

Margin pressure likely to hurt earnings of oil marketing companies (OMCs)

- Consolidated Adjusted Revenue вВє6,665 Cr (вЖС 36% YoY & вЖС 3.6% QoQ)  

- Platform MTUs 25.2 Mn (вЖС 27.2% YoY & вЖС 3.8% QoQ)  

Profitability
- Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA loss вВє652 Cr  
  - Improved by ~вВє80 Cr YoY  
  - Improved by вВє60 Cr QoQ  

- B2C Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to -3.0% of GOV  
  - vs -4.8% YoY  
  - vs -3.5% QoQ  

Food Delivery Business
- Food Delivery GOV вВє9,005 Cr (вЖС 22.6% YoY)  
- Highest growth in 15 quarters  

- Adjusted EBITDA вВє297 Cr (вЖС 39.8% YoY & вЖС 9.1% QoQ)  
- EBITDA Margin 3.3% of GOV  
  - вЖС 41 bps YoY  
  - вЖС 26 bps QoQ  

- MTUs 18.3 Mn (вЖС 21% YoY)  

Quick Commerce вАУ Instamart
- GOV вВє7,881 Cr (вЖС 68.8% YoY)  
- NOV вВє5,675 Cr (вЖС 60.3% YoY & вЖС 4% QoQ)  

- Average Order Value вВє700 (вЖС 33% YoY)  
- Driven by:
  - Higher non-grocery mix  
  - Larger basket purchases  

- Contribution margin improved to -1.8%  
  - Improved 65 bps QoQ  
  - Improved 372 bps YoY  

- March 2026 monthly contribution margin improved further to -1.1%  

- Adjusted EBITDA Loss вВє858 Cr  
  - Reduced by вВє50 Cr QoQ  

- EBITDA Margin -10.9%  
  - Improved 55 bps QoQ  

Dark Store Expansion
- Total darkstore area crossed 4.8 Mn sq ft (вЖС 21.1% YoY)  
- Store count increased to 1,143 across 129 cities  

Out-of-Home Consumption
- Achieved first full year of profitability  
- GOV growth 43% YoY  
- EBITDA margin reached 0.8% of GOV  

Management Commentary
- Food delivery business delivered strongest growth in nearly four years  
- Annual food delivery adjusted EBITDA crossed вВє1,000 Cr  

- Quick commerce unit economics improved every quarter  
- Company remains on track toward contribution margin breakeven  

- Focus shifting from reactive fulfilment to predictive consumer demand models in quick commerce  

- Strong balance sheet provides flexibility and disciplined capital allocation entering FY27  

Final Takeaway
- Swiggy delivered strong growth across food delivery and quick commerce while steadily improving profitability metrics, supported by improving unit economics and expanding consumer engagement  

Impact
- Positive

HYUNDAI MOTOR INDIA FY27 OUTLOOK

Growth Guidance
- Domestic volume growth expected at 8%вАУ10% in FY27  
- Export volume growth also guided at 8%вАУ10%  

Capex Plans
- Planned capital expenditure of around вВє7,500 Cr  
- Investments aimed at:
  - Capacity enhancement  
  - Product development  
  - EV ecosystem expansion  

Margin Guidance
- EBITDA margin expected in the range of 11%вАУ14% for FY27  

Product Launch Pipeline
- Two completely new vehicle nameplates planned in FY27:
  - One focused on strengthening HyundaiвАЩs mid-SUV portfolio  
  - One localized dedicated EV in the compact SUV category  

Strategic Focus
- Expanding SUV portfolio across segments  
- Increasing localization and EV penetration  
- Strengthening domestic and export market positioning  

EV Strategy
- Upcoming compact SUV EV marks HyundaiвАЩs push toward localized electric mobility platform development in India  

Final Takeaway
- Hyundai is entering FY27 with a strong product pipeline, aggressive capex plans and balanced focus on domestic growth, exports and EV expansion  

Impact
- Positive

UJJIVAN SMALL FINANCE BANK Q4FY26

Business Growth
- Gross Loan Book вВє40,655 Cr (вЖС 26.6% YoY & вЖС 9.7% QoQ)  
  - Group Loans вВє14,696 Cr (вЖС 12% YoY & вЖС 7% QoQ)  

- Secured mix improved to 49.4% vs 43.5% YoY & 48.1% QoQ  
- Secured loan book growth (вЖС 44% YoY)  
- Unsecured loan book growth (вЖС 13.5% YoY)  

Disbursements
- Highest-ever quarterly disbursements вВє9,811 Cr (вЖС 32.1% YoY & вЖС 18.3% QoQ)  
- Group Loan disbursements вВє3,870 Cr (вЖС 39% YoY & вЖС 12% QoQ)  

Deposits
- Total Deposits вВє45,668 Cr (вЖС 21.4% YoY & вЖС 8.2% QoQ)  
- CASA Deposits вВє13,062 Cr (вЖС 35.9% YoY)  
- CASA Ratio improved to 28.6%  

Profitability
- NII вВє1,092 Cr (вЖС 26.4% YoY & вЖС 9.2% QoQ)  
- Total Income вВє2,186 Cr (вЖС 19% YoY & вЖС 7% QoQ)  
- PPOP вВє515 Cr (вЖС 43% YoY & вЖС 17% QoQ)  

- Credit Cost вВє144 Cr (вЖУ 46% YoY & вЖУ 26% QoQ)  
- PAT вВє282 Cr (вЖС 238.2% YoY & вЖС 51.6% QoQ)  

Margins & Returns
- NIM 8.5% vs 8.3% YoY & 8.2% QoQ  
- ROA 2.1% vs 0.7% YoY & 1.5% QoQ  
- ROE 17.2% vs 5.6% YoY & 11.5% QoQ  

Asset Quality
- GNPA 2.26% vs 2.18% YoY & 2.38% QoQ  
- NNPA 0.43% vs 0.49% YoY & 0.57% QoQ  
- Portfolio at Risk (PAR) 3.54% vs 4.54% YoY & 3.98% QoQ  
- Collection Efficiency 99.8%  
- Provision Coverage Ratio improved to 81%  

Strategic Developments
- RBI returned application for voluntary transition into Universal Bank  
- RBI acknowledged ongoing diversification efforts  
- Bank plans to reapply after further diversification progress  

Diversification Strategy
- Secured loans now nearly half of total loan book  
- Strong growth in:
  - Gold loans  
  - Vehicle loans  
  - Agriculture loans  

- Newer businesses contribution increased to ~6% of loan mix vs ~3% YoY  

Funding & Capital
- Cost of funds reduced to 7.0% in Q4FY26 (вЖУ 63 bps YoY)  
- Board approved equity raise of up to вВє2,000 Cr to support future growth  

FY27 Guidance
- Loan book growth around 25%  
- Credit Cost guidance: 1.4%вАУ1.5% of average GLB  
- ROA guidance around 1.6%  

Final Takeaway
- Ujjivan delivered strong growth, improving profitability and better asset quality while steadily transitioning toward a more diversified and secured lending franchise aligned with its long-term universal banking ambitions  

Impact
- Positive

LUPIN MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

US Business & FY27 Outlook
- Management expects US business revenue to cross $1 Bn in FY27  
- Injectable portfolio expected to remain a key growth driver  
- Core US portfolio seeing stable demand with volume growth offsetting pricing pressure  

Margin Guidance
- FY27 EBITDA margin guidance maintained around 25%  
- Management confident of sustaining profitability despite external headwinds  

Key Product Performance
- Strong performance from:
  - Tolvaptan  
  - Mirabegron  
  - Spiriva  
  - Albuterol  

- Base products continued to witness healthy volume growth in the US market  

Margin Headwinds
- Management highlighted possible pressure from:
  - Higher air freight costs  
  - Elevated ocean freight expenses  
  - Logistics-related disruptions in global markets  

Semaglutide Update
- Only 9 days of Semaglutide sales contributed in Q4FY26  
- April sales trend reported as healthy  
- Early rollout witnessing strong traction in India  

International Expansion
- Semaglutide launch in Canada expected in FY28  
- Company evaluating two large acquisitions focused on:
  - India business  
  - Specialty segment expansion  

Strategic Focus
- Continued push toward:
  - Specialty products  
  - Injectables  
  - Biosimilars  
  - Complex generics  

- Pegfilgrastim launch planned in biosimilars segment  

Final Takeaway
- Lupin is entering FY27 with strong momentum in the US business, improving specialty mix and early success in Semaglutide, while maintaining focus on margins and global expansion opportunities  

Impact
- Positive

INDIA POWER SECTOR FY27 OUTLOOK

Demand Outlook
- FY27 electricity demand growth projected at 5% to 5.5%  
- Sharp recovery from around 1% growth in FY26  
- FY26 demand impacted by weather related disruptions and lower thermal utilisation  

Key Demand Drivers
- Higher agricultural and household consumption due to possible sub-par rainfall and El Ni√±o risk  
- Rising industrial activity  
- Growing EV charging infrastructure demand  
- Increasing electricity consumption from data centres  

Thermal Power Revival
- Around 50 GW generation capacity addition expected in FY27  
- Includes nearly 6 GW thermal capacity; balance from renewables  
- Under-construction thermal capacity crossed 45 GW after years of subdued investment  

Thermal PLF Trend
- FY21: 54.5%  
- FY24: 69.1%  
- FY25: 69.5%  
- FY26: 65.7%  
- FY27E: ~65.5%  

- PLFs expected to remain stable despite demand rebound due to fresh thermal additions and renewable penetration  

Investment and Project Activity
- PSU and private sector thermal project announcements increasing  
- State discoms restarting long-term power purchase bidding activity  
- Thermal power regaining importance as reliable base-load generation for grid stability  

Execution Challenges
- Long project gestation periods remain a key risk  
- Boiler, turbine and generator manufacturers operating with elevated order books  

Electricity Pricing Trends
- Average IEX DAM spot tariff declined to вВє3.8/unit in FY26 from вВє4.4/unit in FY25  
- Softer prices due to slower demand growth and higher renewable generation  

Coal and Fuel Position
- Coal stock levels at thermal plants remain comfortable at around 19 days  
- Supported by improved domestic coal availability  

Discom Financial Stress
- Aggregate discom debt declined modestly to вВє7.1 lakh Cr  
- Cash gap still elevated at 30вАУ33 paise per unit  
- Tariff hikes across states remain muted  
- Tariff orders issued in only 17 of 28 states by April 2026  

Key Structural Concerns
- Rising power purchase costs  
- Slow smart metering implementation  
- Weak operational efficiency in distribution segment  
- Limited tariff pass-through mechanisms  

Sector Theme
- IndiaвАЩs power sector is entering a dual-cycle phase:
  - Strong generation demand recovery led by EVs, industry and data centres  
  - Continued financial stress at discom level limiting sector-wide monetisation efficiency  

Final Takeaway
- Power generation and equipment ecosystem likely to benefit from rising demand and thermal revival, while structural discom weakness remains the biggest long-term risk for sector sustainability  

Impact
- Positive for generation, transmission and equipment players  
- Cautious on distribution segment

KPI GREEN ENERGY Q4 HIGHLIGHTS

Capacity Expansion
- Total portfolio expanded from 3.91 GW in FY25 to 6.26 GW in FY26  
- Includes installed and work-in-progress capacity  

Segment-wise Capacity

IPP Segment
- Installed capacity: 0.96 GW  
- WIP capacity: 1.61 GW  

CPP Segment
- Installed capacity: 0.66 GW  
- WIP capacity: 3.03 GW  

Major Order Wins
- 300 MW Wind Power Project from SJVN  
- 120 MW / 240 MWh IPP project from GUVNL  
- 445 MW / 890 MWh BESS order  
- 150 MW Wind Power Project with GUVNL  
- 1 TPD Plasma Green Hydrogen plant  
- 142 MW Floating Solar Photovoltaic project  
- 250 MW Solar Power Project from SJVN  
- 1.13 GW Solar Power Project from Adani Group  

Strategic Positioning
- Increasing focus on:
  - Utility scale renewable projects  
  - Government linked contracts  
  - Advanced clean energy infrastructure  
  - Strategic energy transition opportunities  

Future Growth Areas
- Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)  
- Energy trading licences  
- Green hydrogen projects  
- Floating solar projects  
- Offshore wind power  
- Pumped storage projects  

Business Transformation
- Evolving from solar EPC player to vertically integrated renewable energy and infrastructure platform  
- Building capabilities across generation, storage and energy solutions ecosystem  

Final Takeaway
- Strong order inflow, diversified renewable pipeline and expansion into storage and hydrogen position KPI Green as an emerging integrated clean energy platform  

Impact
- Positive

WAAREE ENERGY STRATEGIC EXPANSION UPDATE

Long-Term Vision
- Management targeting revenue of вВє1,00,000 Cr within the next 5 years  
- FY26 revenue base around вВє26,500 Cr  
- Transition underway from solar module manufacturer to integrated clean energy infrastructure company  

Business Transformation
- Earlier focus primarily on solar modules  
- Now scaling across:
  - Solar glass  
  - Wafers  
  - Cells  
  - Batteries  
  - Energy Storage Systems (ESS)  
  - Inverters  
  - Hydrogen equipment  

Strategic Goal
- Aim to capture nearly 90% of customer wallet share across the clean energy value chain  
- Building vertically integrated ecosystem to improve margins, scale and customer stickiness  

US Expansion Opportunity
- US market emerging as a major growth driver due to:
  - IRA incentives  
  - Rising AI and data center power demand  
  - Industrial electrification trends  

Capacity Targets
- Targeting 15.4 GW cell manufacturing capacity  
- Targeting 20 GWh battery business by 2028  

Industry Positioning
- Moving beyond solar manufacturing into broader energy infrastructure and storage ecosystem  
- Positioned to benefit from:
  - Renewable energy growth  
  - Grid-scale storage demand  
  - Decarbonization initiatives globally  

Final Takeaway
- Waaree is pursuing an aggressive full-stack clean energy integration strategy with significant optionality across solar, storage and energy infrastructure segments  

Impact
- Positive

Escorts Kubota: Guidance - Concall Update

вАҐ Management expects the tractor industry for FY27 to be flattish, with a potential range of 2-3% up or down, anticipating a subdued H2 due to high base effects, subnormal monsoon forecasts, and El Nino impact.

вАҐ The company is confident of achieving positive growth and market share improvement in tractor volumes for FY27, irrespective of the industry outlook, driven by recent product launches and channel development.

вАҐ The non-tractor agri segment (farm implements) is expected to grow at a 20%+ CAGR over the next three years, supported by product pipeline expansion and channel growth.

вАҐ Commodity cost increases are anticipated to be around 5-6% as a percentage of revenue, with a 1.5% price hike already implemented on tractors and construction equipment, which is insufficient to cover the expected increases.

вАҐ Normal CapEx for FY27 is projected at INR350-400 crores, with an additional INR500 crores for the greenfield facility this year, part of a larger INR5,000+ crore investment over 7-10 years.

вАҐ An additional INR500 crores will be invested in the captive finance division over the next 12-15 months, following an initial INR200 crore investment, with a long-term ROA aspiration of 1.5-2%.

вАҐ The Construction Equipment segment anticipates short-term challenges due to macro factors but expects a turnaround in H2 FY27 and strong medium-term demand from public infrastructure development.

Blue Star: Guidance - Concall Update

Management is cautiously optimistic for FY27, with the outlook heavily dependent on the summer season's duration and intensity.

For Segment 1 (Electro-Mechanical Projects and Commercial Air Conditioning), the outlook for Commercial AC is 8% to 10% growth, driven by manufacturing, while data center MEP business is expected to more than double within three years, potentially growing from INR1,000 crore to INR3,000 crore annually.

 The company aims to maintain Segment 1 margins at 7% to 7.5% and Segment 2 (Unitary Products) margins at 8% to 8.5% for the current financial year, despite anticipated margin pressure from rising input costs and volatile exchange rates.

Annual capital expenditure is projected to be in the range of INR250 crores to INR350 crores, covering routine CapEx, R&D, product development, and IT/digital investments.

The Room Air Conditioner industry is expected to grow at an 18% to 20% CAGR, potentially reaching 40 million to 50 million units by 2030, with FY27 projected at 17.5 million units, but margins will remain under pressure due to competition and commodity prices.

Radico Khaitan: Guidance - Concall Update

Co achieved net revenue exceeding INR6,000 crores and EBITDA crossing INR1,000 crores in FY'26, with the luxury portfolio delivering INR475 crores in sales value, in line with guidance.

For FY'27, the company expects its luxury portfolio to sustain 25% growth and the Prestige & Above portfolio volume to grow by 20%.

EBITDA margin is projected to expand by 125 basis points for the full year FY'27, driven by price increases (60 bps) and product premiumization (over 200 bps), despite potential commodity cost impacts.

Co aims to become debt-free in FY'27 and has articulated a dividend policy with a minimum payout of 20% of profit after tax.

For the regular segment, a volume growth of 3% to 5% is expected for FY'27, while the non-IMFL business is anticipated to grow at 7% to 8%.

ONE 97 COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE UPDATE

FY27 Outlook
- Management expects revenue growth acceleration in FY27  
- Growth to be driven by:
  - Payments business momentum  
  - Financial services expansion  
  - Recovery in marketing services  

Profitability Targets
- Targeting EBITDA margins of 15% to 20% over the next 2.5 to 3 years  
- Operating leverage expected to improve steadily  

Cost Structure
- Indirect costs expected to grow significantly lower than:
  - Revenue growth  
  - Contribution profit growth  

- Focus remains on improving efficiency and scalability  

Customer Acquisition Strategy
- Promotional and cashback spending to continue in a calibrated manner  
- Incentives will remain within existing ratio framework  
- Focus on:
  - High quality customer acquisition  
  - User engagement improvement  

AI Strategy
- Incremental investments planned in AI capabilities  
- AI to enhance customer and merchant experience across existing businesses  
- No major capital expenditure planned for data centers  

Management View
- Strong long-term confidence in payments and financial services ecosystem  
- AI driven operational enhancement expected to support profitability and engagement  

Final Takeaway
- Paytm is focusing on profitable growth through operating leverage, AI integration and scaling financial services while maintaining disciplined customer acquisition spending  

Impact
- Positive

PNC INFRATECH вАУ PROJECT UPDATE

вАҐ Declared L1 bidder for Lucknow flyover EPC project by Lucknow Development Authority

вАҐ Project includes construction of 4-lane flyover with 2 loops & 2 ramps at Shaheed Path Intersection, Gomti River RHS bank

вАҐ Quoted bid value at вВє194.4 Cr

вАҐ Execution timeline set at 24 months

вАҐ Order strengthens infrastructure order book & revenue visibility

DREDGING CORPORATION вАУ GREEN ENERGY UPDATE

вАҐ Commissioned 150 kW rooftop solar plant at Visakhapatnam headquarters under PM Surya Ghar Yojana

вАҐ Plant expected to generate ~22,500 units per month and ~2.7 lakh units annually

вАҐ Estimated annual power cost savings around вВє30 lakh

вАҐ Project expected to reduce 220вАУ240 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually

вАҐ Initiative supports sustainability, green energy adoption & operational efficiency

COCHIN SHIPYARD вАУ ORDER WIN

вАҐ Udupi Cochin Shipyard (wholly owned subsidiary) secured вАШNotableвАЩ domestic order from Ocean Sparkle Ltd (Adani Group)

вАҐ Order involves construction of 4 ASD Tugboats with 70T Bollard Pull capacity

вАҐ Vessels to be built under approved standard tug design norms of Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways

вАҐ Deliveries scheduled from November 2028 to July 2029

вАҐ Order falls under вАШNotableвАЩ category, implying value between вВє100вАУ250 Cr

вАҐ Order strengthens long-term order book visibility in specialised shipbuilding segment

Capex Amount for 1GW

Module- 100-150Cr 
Cell+ Module- 500-600Cr
Ingot&Wafer +Cell+ Module- 1100-1200Cr
Poly to Module- 1500-1600Cr 

Capital Intensity increases as you go up from Just Module Manufacturer to Integrated Player.
10x More Capex Heavy than just Module. 

India first incentivesed Module Manufactures, Then Cell and Now moving towards Ingot & Wafer as well. 

Those who will be integrated player, only will be able to catch this train.

*~~Citi*

*India Electric Utilities: Switched-On Capex Cycle: Initiate NTPC, PGCIL, TTPW, JSWE at Buy*

IndiaвАЩs first-ever multi-vector capex upcycle вАУ straddling thermal, renewables, transmission, and grid storage вАУ continues to chug along. It remains a multi-year visible story, with the breadth of power demand gradually expanding. Our medium-term forecast is set at a 5-6% CAGR on the strength of electrification, data centres, cooling load, and policy-supported manufacturing sectors. To boot, regulatorsвАЩ focus has pivoted from capacity to reliability/flexibility, and favorable policies are providing greater visibility. Against this structural change in backdrop, we initiate at Buy on NTPC (TP Rs485), TTPW (Rs525), PGRD (Rs380), JSWE (Rs650) вАУ in that order of preference.

*Stock views: NTPC > Tata Power >PGRD > JSWE вАФ Although power-sector stocks have re-rated in the past month on a power-demand pickup, we believe they still have upside potential. None are materially exposed to merchant price volatility.*

- *NTPC:* Duration/diversity of its regulated business and its multi-modal generation exposure with a cost of funds advantage position it well.

- *Tata Power:* Multi-year transition, with a growing share of lower-volatility businesses as capex stays focused on T&D and renewables.

- *PGRD:* Direct beneficiary of transmission grid buildout in India. Commissioning and capex trajectories have improved.

- *JSWE:* Well placed to benefit from both thermal and renewable buildout.

*Key risks* вАФ a) Execution slippages; b) sustained unfavorable demand variability; c) adverse regulatory developments.

IndiaвАЩs Energy Edge: Optionality

IndiaвАЩs energy security is no longer about self-sufficiency; itвАЩs about strategic optionality. Importing over 85% of its crude, India has navigated the West Asia crisis by diversifying its basketвАФshifting from Middle East reliance to making Russia its largest supplier (36%). While Brent crude hovers near $109, IndiaвАЩs ability to pivot between Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and the US has provided a vital macroeconomic cushion.

However, the "new oil" order brings fresh risks. As we transition toward EVs and solar, our dependence moves from oil chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz to critical mineral supply chains dominated by China. Resilience now requires more than just filling strategic reserves; it demands securing the lithium and cobalt needed for the next decade of growth. Diversification is our only shield in a fragmented market.

reports.text:
SEIL Receives 5-Year Renewal of MES Approval

Steel Exchange India Ltd. has received renewal of approval from the Military Engineer Services (MES) under the Ministry of Defence for supply of SIMHADRI TMT bars for the next 5 years.

Update Highlights:
* Approval renewed for Fe 500D and Fe 500D HCRM TMT bars from 8 mm to 32 mm.
* Products are manufactured at SEILвАЩs integrated Vizianagaram plant using TEMPCORE technology.
* Renewal follows MES quality checks, technical evaluations, and inspection standards.
* MES approvals are granted only to manufacturers meeting stringent quality requirements.
* Approval remains subject to periodic inspections and IS 1786:2008 compliance.
* SEIL continues to remain eligible for MES-linked infrastructure and defence projects.

Strategic Alignment:
The renewal strengthens SEILвАЩs position in institutional and government-linked segments while reinforcing its focus on quality standards, operational discipline, and participation in high entry-barrier projects.

reports.text:
Thailand's Land Bridge: A Game-Changer for Global Trade?

Thailand is pushing ahead with its ambitious $30B+ Land Bridge вАФ a 90km multimodal corridor linking the Andaman Sea to the Gulf of Thailand. The goal? Create a faster, cheaper overland shortcut that bypasses the congested and vulnerable Strait of Malacca, shaving 4-5 days off shipping times and ~15% off costs.

In an era of Red Sea/Hormuz disruptions and growing worries about maritime chokepoints, this project isn't just infrastructure вАФ it's strategic resilience. If executed well, it could reposition Thailand as a key logistics hub in the Indo-Pacific, challenging Singapore's dominance and reshaping supply chains.

Decades in discussion, now gaining real momentum. The big questions remain: execution, environment, and geopolitics. Worth watching closely.

Join our WhatsApp Channel. рЯЩПрЯПї

рЯСЙ https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VbBm3EiKWEKxIsMdoz2X

Politicians: The Demographic of Disconnect

While the common man pours into SIPs, IndiaвАЩs political class remains allergic to the demat account. Out of вВє29,032 crore in disclosed assets across five states, a staggering 99% is locked in land, buildings, and gold. Only a minuscule 0.97% sits in regulated capital marketsвАФa stark contrast to US Congress members who often hold 50-80% of their wealth in stocks.

The data reveals a "sideline" mentality: only 461 out of 7,325 candidates disclosed any market exposure. For a leadership pushing a "Viksit Bharat" built on financialization, their personal portfolios tell a different story. They aren't betting on IndiaвАЩs corporate growth; they are betting on the soil beneath it. The biggest underserved market for financial advisors? The very people making the policies.

GOLDMAN SACHS ON TATA TECHNOLOGIES

Target price revised to вВє470 from вВє450

Q4 results in-line with estimates

Management maintained guidance for double-digit organic revenue growth in FY27

Introduced FY27 EBITDA margin guidance of 18% versus 15.7% in FY26

Brokerage expects margin improvement from better utilization and Al-led efficiencies

Says Middle East conflict unlikely to impact auto OEM capex plans materially

Raises FY27/FY29 EPS estimates by 10%/6%

ELARA CAPITAL ON JYOTHY LABS

Maintain Buy, Target price: вВє335 versus 320

Q4 revenue growth driven by fabric care and personal care segments

Volume growth came in strong at 10.8%

Margins remained under pressure due to crude-linked input cost inflation

Gross margin contracted 407 bps YoY while EBITDA margin declined 335 bps YoY

Brokerage cuts FY27 EPS estimate by 19.3% due to margin pressure

Maintains positive stance citing long-term growth visibility

MORGAN STANLEY ON KEI INDUSTRIES

Downgrade to Equal-weight from Overweight

Target price: вВє5,213 versus вВє4,860

Q4 PAT beat estimates led by stronger margins despite softer volume growth

Domestic C&W revenue grew 23% while exports remained weak

Brokerage sees revenue growth supported largely by higher commodity prices and rupee depreciation

Warns of margin pressure from rising competition in wires segment

Cuts FY27/FY28 EPS estimates by 3-4%

Says risk-reward now balanced after 35% outperformance versus Sensex in six months

MORGAN STANLEY ON ADITYA BIRLA CAPITAL

Maintain Overweight, Target price: вВє408 versus вВє405

NBFC business delivered strong loan growth and asset quality improvement

Life insurance business posted VNB and margin beat

Loan growth accelerated to 27% YoY across segments

Management expects gradual NIM improvement den and targets ~2.5% ROA by Q4FY27

Brokerage sees healthy upside supported by attractive valuations

Target price revised marginally higher on earnings tweaks

ELARA CAPITAL ON AMBUJA CEMENTS

Maintain Accumulate, Target price: вВє494 versus вВє591

Q4 EBITDA missed estimates sharply amid elevated costs and weak operating efficiency

EBITDA per tonne declined to вВє736

Management recalibrated expansion plans and deferred long-term capacity target timeline

Focus shifts toward cost reduction and improving asset productivity

Brokerage cuts FY27/FY28 EBITDA estimates by 31%/23%

Target price lowered on weaker earnings outlook

MOTILAL OSWAL ON MANAPPURAM FINANCE

Maintain Neutral with a target price of вВє315 (earlier вВє290)

Company reported a mixed performance

Strong gold loan growth, but NIMs remain under pressure

Yields expected to stabilise, while non-gold portfolio asset quality concerns persist

Over FY26-28, estimate a CAGR of 25% (gold AUM) / 23% (consolidated AUM) and ~66% in consolidated PAT, with ROA/ROE of ~3% / 14% in FY28

Gold loans nearly doubled in FY26; gold tonnage up 7% QoQ

Margins under near-term pressure, with stabilisation expected ahead

NIMS projected at ~10.1% /~10.2% for FY27E / FY28E

Asirvad MFI shows early signs of recovery with improving collections

Asset quality in vehicle finance and the recovery trajectory of Asirvad MFI remain key monitorables
